result:
Done. The work is committed (`589677b`).

## What I built

A zero-dependency JSON REST API for a `notes` resource in Node.js (ESM):

- **`src/db.js`** — persistence layer using the built-in `node:sqlite` (`DatabaseSync`), writing to an on-disk file at `data/notes.db`. Prepared statements for create/list/get/update/delete; auto-incrementing `id` and ISO `createdAt` assigned server-side. Path overridable via `DB_PATH`.
- **`src/server.js`** — plain `node:http` router implementing all five endpoints:
  - `POST /notes` → validates `title`/`body` are non-empty strings → `201` with the created note, else `400`
  - `GET /notes` → `200` array of all notes
  - `GET /notes/:id` → `200` note or `404`
  - `PUT /notes/:id` → accepts partial `title`/`body`, `200` updated / `400` invalid / `404` absent
  - `DELETE /notes/:id` → `204` or `404`
  - Listens on `PORT` (default 3000). Guarded so importing it for tests doesn't auto-listen; also handles malformed JSON and oversized bodies.
- **`package.json`** — added `start` (`node src/server.js`) and `test` scripts.
- **`test/notes.test.js`** — full `node:test` suite covering every endpoint and error case.
- **README** documents endpoints; `.gitignore` excludes the `data/` DB dir.

## Verification

- `npm test` → **6/6 passing**, covering 201/200/400/404/204 paths.
- **Persistence across restart confirmed manually**: created a note, killed the server, restarted it, and `GET /notes/:id` returned the same note.

Only my new files were committed; the unrelated pre-existing `.agents/` working-tree deletions were left untouched.
